metadata.summary: AI coding agents must post a structured spec comment (current/desired/plan) on the ticket before touching any code. Implementation only begins after explicit human approval signal.

content.title: Agent Drafts Spec Before Coding
content.summary: When an AI coding agent is assigned a tracker ticket (Linear, Jira, GitHub, GitLab), its first action MUST be a "current state / desired state / proposed plan" comment posted back on the issue, not a code edit. The agent gathers the issue body plus linked context (Notion spec, Slack thread, prior similar issues, code-intel search) and emits a structured spec draft. Implementation only begins after a specialist signals approval — Linear thumbs-up emoji on the agent's comment, Jira /agent approve slash command, or an explicit agent:approved label transition. First-try-pass on coding (no spec gate) means the agent skipped the discovery step and is fabricating intent.

why.p: April 2026 production data from Linear, Atlassian Rovo, GitHub Copilot Workspace, and GitLab Duo all converge on the same gate: agents that draft a spec comment first land mergeable PRs at materially higher rates than agents that jump to a diff. The mechanism is twofold — (a) the agent is forced to surface its interpretation of "done", which catches misreadings cheaply (a comment is rolled back with a reaction, a wrong PR costs a review cycle); (b) the spec doubles as a permanent audit artefact attached to the ticket, satisfying the same reviewability requirement that branch-protection rules enforce on the diff side. Linear's own engineering team and GitHub's Copilot Workspace both encode this as the first stage of their agent contract.

list.item: Ticket is assigned to an AI coding agent on Linear, Jira, GitHub, or GitLab as the primary owner of the work.
Issue has any non-trivial scope: feature work, multi-file bug fix, refactor, migration.
Team has at least one human reviewer who can react with a thumbs-up or run an approval slash command.
Workflow auto-closes the issue on PR merge (Fixes #N, Closes !N) — the spec comment becomes the linkable record of intent.
Trivial tickets pre-flagged agent:auto-approve (typo fixes, dependency bumps, log-message tweaks) — gate cost exceeds risk.
Spike or research tickets where the desired state is itself the deliverable; the agent's job is to discover, not to commit code.
Hot-path incident response where a postmortem ticket is filed after the fact; spec drafting belongs to the retro, not the fix.
Single-author solo projects with no second human in the loop — the gate has no approver and the agent stalls.

section.name: spec-comment-contract

rule.statement: On ticket assignment the agent MUST post exactly one comment containing the four sections in this order: Current State, Desired State, Proposed Plan (INVEST-decomposed steps), Open Questions. The comment MUST mention the issue creator. The agent MUST NOT touch the source tree until the approval gate fires; any pre-approval write to a tracked file fails the lint and the cycle aborts.
rule.rationale: Linear's own internal use of its agent and GitHub Copilot Workspace's spec→plan→diff→PR contract both treat the first-comment spec as the unit of human review; without it, reviewers must reconstruct intent from the diff, which is the exact cost the gate eliminates.

example.code:
@reporter — drafted plan for review:
**Current State.** `POST /api/orders` returns 200 even when `items=[]`; downstream invoice job crashes (LIN-4421).
**Desired State.** Empty `items` returns 422 with `{"error":"items_required"}`; existing flows unchanged.
**Proposed Plan.** 1. Add `items: NonEmptyList` to `OrderRequestSchema` (1 file, ~3 lines). 2. Map ValidationError to 422 in `orders/views.py`. 3. Add regression test `test_order_empty_items_rejected`.
**Open Questions.** Confirm 422 (vs 400) is the agreed status. Tag @api-owner if otherwise.
example.note: Each section short, plan steps small enough to be testable, mention forces the reporter to react.

example.code: I'll fix the empty-items bug. Opening a PR shortly.
example.note: No current/desired split, no plan, no open question — reviewer cannot react with a thumbs-up because there is nothing to approve.

antipattern.description: Agent posts the spec comment AND opens a draft PR in the same turn.
antipattern.reason: The PR consumes a review slot before the spec is approved; reviewers anchor on the diff and skip the cheap correction the comment was supposed to enable.

section.reference: Markdown skeleton the agent fills in.

see-also.link: Linear engineering: agent posts checkpoints + PR link as comments.
GitHub Copilot Workspace four-stage gate: spec, plan, diff, PR.

section.name: approval-gate-mechanics

rule.statement: The agent MUST treat the gate as cleared only when an approval event originates from an identity NOT matching ^(claude|copilot|devin|cursor|rovo|duo|bot)-.*$ and is one of: Linear :thumbsup: reaction on the agent's spec comment, Jira issue comment matching ^/agent\s+approve\b, or GitHub issue labelled agent:approved by a user with write access. Self-approval, agent-to-agent approval, and approval before the spec comment is posted MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Atlassian's "agents respect your permissions, project configurations, workflows, and audit trails" and GitHub's branch-protection-style separation of approver from author both encode the same invariant: an automated actor cannot satisfy a gate that exists to bound automation.

example.code: # templates/approval-gate.yaml (excerpt) approval: linear: event: reaction.added on_comment: agent_spec_comment_id emoji: thumbsup actor_filter: human_only jira: event: comment.created body_match: '^/agent\s+approve\b' actor_filter: project_role_developer github: event: issue.labeled label: agent:approved actor_filter: write_permission
example.note: Each tracker maps to one event with an actor filter; the agent's proceed only fires on the union.
A second agent (or the same agent on a stale comment) trips the gate; nothing prevents self-approval.

antipattern.description: Agent treats absence of dissent (no comments for N minutes) as implicit approval.
antipattern.reason: "Silence = consent" reliably ships wrong-spec PRs when the reporter is asleep, on PTO, or in a different timezone; the gate must be an explicit positive signal.

section.reference: Per-tracker approval mapping consumed by the agent runtime.

see-also.link: Linear: agents as first-class assignees with comment-based handoff.
Atlassian: agents inherit Jira permissions, workflows, audit trails.
GitHub community: enforcing the link/label contract on agent PRs.

template.purpose: Markdown skeleton the agent fills in for the first ticket comment.
Per-tracker config mapping the approval signal to the agent's proceed event.
